{{Fireworks Festival |name=Nanbu Fire Festival |english_name= |image=[[File:南部の火祭り 船灯籠 - Panoramio 57392817.jpg|300px]] |caption=Boat lanterns at the Nanbu Fire Festival |alias= |official_name=Nanbu Fire Festival |former_name= |schedule=[[August 15]] (previously [[August 16]] during the Shōwa era) |first_event=[[1960]] (Shōwa 35), August 16 |venue={{ill|Nanbu, Yamanashi|ja|南部町 (山梨県)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fuji River|ja|富士川|12=draft}} riverside near {{ill|Nanbu Bridge (Fuji River)|ja|南部橋 (富士川)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Minamikoma District, Yamanashi|ja|南巨摩郡|12=draft}} |fireworks_count=3,000 |organizer=Nanbu Fire Festival Executive Committee |support= |sponsors= |cooperation= |management= |firework_type=Ni-shakudama (two-foot diameter shell) |attendance=40,000 |attendance_year=2014 |nearest_station=[[JR Central]] {{ill|Minobu Line|ja|身延線|12=draft}}, {{ill|Utsubuna Station|ja|内船駅|12=draft}} |shuttle_bus= |url=http://www.town.nanbu.yamanashi.jp/kankou/omatsuri/himatsuri/yurai.html |external_link_name=About the Nanbu Fire Festival |remarks= }} '''Nanbu Fire Festival''' (南部の火祭り) is a traditional summer festival held in {{ill|Nanbu, Yamanashi|ja|南部町 (山梨県)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Minamikoma District, Yamanashi|ja|南巨摩郡|12=draft}}, during the {{ill|Obon|ja|お盆|12=draft}} season.
== Main Events == ;Torch Throwing Participants aim torches at a "beehive" (a straw-filled basket) mounted atop a tall pole, spinning and throwing the torches to ignite it. Once burned, the pole is shaken to release the remains. The tradition is similar to the {{ill|Karigane Festival|ja|かりがね祭り|12=draft}} held in {{ill|Fuji, Shizuoka|ja|富士市|12=draft}}. Before World War II, this event spanned three nights (August 14–16) and used split cedar logs tied with bamboo hoops. Modern torches are made from reused cloth soaked in waste oil or kerosene and secured with wire. ;Giant Torches Old tōba (wooden memorial plaques) from local temples are stacked to create large torches. These are lit around 7 PM following sutra chanting by monks. ;One Hundred and Eight Fires Symbolizing the cleansing of humanity's 108 earthly desires (bonnō), the ritual also serves as a memorial for the dead and a pest control measure to drive away rice planthopper pests (unja). Cone-shaped bonfires are placed along both banks of the {{ill|Fuji River|ja|富士川|12=draft}}, covering approximately 2 km. All 108 fires are lit simultaneously at 8 PM following a siren. ;Fireworks Following the lighting of the 108 fires, a fireworks display featuring 3,000 shells, including two-foot diameter shells, illuminates the sky. ;Additional Activities The main venue on the Mutsumiai side features events such as karaoke contests and food stalls. However, due to financial constraints in Nanbu Town, the scale of the festival has been reduced in recent years.
== Transportation == ;Rail * To the Sakae-side venue: A 2-minute walk from {{ill|Utsubuna Station|ja|内船駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Minobu Line|ja|身延線|12=draft}}. Temporary trains operate during the festival, similar to those for the {{ill|Shinmei Fireworks Festival|ja|神明の花火大会|12=draft}}. Food stalls offering classics like yakisoba, takoyaki, and jumbo sausages are present, but additional snacks and drinks can be purchased at nearby convenience stores (e.g., a Daily Yamazaki) or local shops. * To the Mutsumiai-side main venue: A 10-minute walk from Utsubuna Station, crossing the {{ill|Nanbu Bridge (Fuji River)|ja|南部橋 (富士川)|12=draft}}. While the 2011 completion tier 2]][[Category: of a new bridge widened the pedestrian path, traffic restrictions may apply during busy times. * Additional services: Temporary trains or extended train schedules are available between {{ill|Fuji Station|ja|富士駅|12=draft}}, Utsubuna Station, and {{ill|Minobu Station|ja|身延駅|12=draft}}. Temporary train schedules are typically not listed in commercial timetables but are announced via {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|12=draft}}'s official website or flyers distributed around the same time as the Shinmei Fireworks Festival announcements. ;Car * Guidance signs are posted along {{ill|National Route 52 (Japan)|ja|国道52号|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shizuoka-Yamanashi Prefectural Route 10|ja|静岡県道・山梨県道10号富士川身延線|12=draft}}. Parking is available near the main venue on the Mutsumiai side but requires using designated lots due to traffic restrictions. Parking areas include locations near Utsubuna Station and along the Fuji River embankments. Note that the parking lot at Utsubuna Station, usually free, charges fees on the festival day.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Nanbu Fire Festival}} * [http://www.town.nanbu.yamanashi.jp/kankou/omatsuri/himatsuri/yurai.html Nanbu Fire Festival (Nanbu Town Hall)] <!--Nambu Fire Festival-->

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Nanbu Fire Festival]][[ja:南部の火祭り]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11408485]] Wikidata name: Nanbu Fire Festival [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Fireworks Festival |name=Nanbu Fire Festival |english_name= |image=[[File:南部の火祭り 船灯籠 - Panoramio 57392817.jpg|300px]] |caption=Boat lanterns at the Nanbu Fire Festival |alias= |official_name=Nanbu Fire Festival |former_name= |schedule=[[August 15]] (previously [[August 16]] during the Shōwa era) |first_event=[[1960]] (Shōwa 35), August 16 |venue=[[:en:Nanbu, Yamanashi|Nanbu, Yamanashi]], [[:en:Fuji River|Fuji River]] riverside near {{ill|Nanbu Bridge (Fuji River)|ja|南部橋 (富士川)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanbu Bridge (Fuji River)|qq=|1=Nanbu bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11408565}}, [[:en:Minamikoma District, Yamanashi|Minamikoma District, Yamanashi]] |fireworks_count=3,000 |organizer=Nanbu Fire Festival Executive Committee |support= |sponsors= |cooperation= |management= |firework_type=Ni-shakudama (two-foot diameter shell) |attendance=40,000 |attendance_year=2014 |nearest_station=[[JR Central]] [[:en:Minobu Line|Minobu Line]], [[:en:Utsubuna Station|Utsubuna Station]] |shuttle_bus= |url=http://www.town.nanbu.yamanashi.jp/kankou/omatsuri/himatsuri/yurai.html |external_link_name=About the Nanbu Fire Festival |remarks= }} '''Nanbu Fire Festival''' (南部の火祭り) is a traditional summer festival held in [[:en:Nanbu, Yamanashi|Nanbu, Yamanashi]], [[:en:Minamikoma District, Yamanashi|Minamikoma District, Yamanashi]], during the [[:en:Obon|Obon]] season.
== Main Events == ;Torch Throwing Participants aim torches at a "beehive" (a straw-filled basket) mounted atop a tall pole, spinning and throwing the torches to ignite it. Once burned, the pole is shaken to release the remains. The tradition is similar to the {{ill|Karigane Festival|ja|かりがね祭り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karigane Festival|qq=|1=Karigane Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11264366}} held in [[:en:Fuji, Shizuoka|Fuji, Shizuoka]]. Before World War II, this event spanned three nights (August 14–16) and used split cedar logs tied with bamboo hoops. Modern torches are made from reused cloth soaked in waste oil or kerosene and secured with wire. ;Giant Torches Old tōba (wooden memorial plaques) from local temples are stacked to create large torches. These are lit around 7 PM following sutra chanting by monks. ;One Hundred and Eight Fires Symbolizing the cleansing of humanity's 108 earthly desires (bonnō), the ritual also serves as a memorial for the dead and a pest control measure to drive away rice planthopper pests (unja). Cone-shaped bonfires are placed along both banks of the [[:en:Fuji River|Fuji River]], covering approximately 2 km. All 108 fires are lit simultaneously at 8 PM following a siren. ;Fireworks Following the lighting of the 108 fires, a fireworks display featuring 3,000 shells, including two-foot diameter shells, illuminates the sky. ;Additional Activities The main venue on the Mutsumiai side features events such as karaoke contests and food stalls. However, due to financial constraints in Nanbu Town, the scale of the festival has been reduced in recent years.
== Transportation == ;Rail * To the Sakae-side venue: A 2-minute walk from [[:en:Utsubuna Station|Utsubuna Station]] on the [[:en:Minobu Line|Minobu Line]]. Temporary trains operate during the festival, similar to those for the {{ill|Shinmei Fireworks Festival|ja|神明の花火大会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei Fireworks Festival|qq=|1=Shinmei Fireworks Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590283}}. Food stalls offering classics like yakisoba, takoyaki, and jumbo sausages are present, but additional snacks and drinks can be purchased at nearby convenience stores (e.g., a Daily Yamazaki) or local shops. * To the Mutsumiai-side main venue: A 10-minute walk from Utsubuna Station, crossing the {{ill|Nanbu Bridge (Fuji River)|ja|南部橋 (富士川)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanbu Bridge (Fuji River)|qq=|1=Nanbu bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11408565}}. While the 2011 Completion tier 2]][[Category: of a new bridge widened the pedestrian path, traffic restrictions may apply during busy times. * Additional services: Temporary trains or extended train schedules are available between [[:en:Fuji Station|Fuji Station]], Utsubuna Station, and [[:en:Minobu Station|Minobu Station]]. Temporary train schedules are typically not listed in commercial timetables but are announced via [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]'s official website or flyers distributed around the same time as the Shinmei Fireworks Festival announcements. ;Car * Guidance signs are posted along [[:en:Japan National Route 52|National Route 52 (Japan)]] and {{ill|Shizuoka-Yamanashi Prefectural Route 10|ja|静岡県道・山梨県道10号富士川身延線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuoka-Yamanashi Prefectural Route 10|qq=|1=Shizuoka Prefectural Road and Yamanashi Prefectural Road Route 10|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11663557}}. Parking is available near the main venue on the Mutsumiai side but requires using designated lots due to traffic restrictions. Parking areas include locations near Utsubuna Station and along the Fuji River embankments. Note that the parking lot at Utsubuna Station, usually free, charges fees on the festival day.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Nanbu Fire Festival}} * [http://www.town.nanbu.yamanashi.jp/kankou/omatsuri/himatsuri/yurai.html Nanbu Fire Festival (Nanbu Town Hall)]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Nanbu Fire Festival]][[ja:南部の火祭り]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11408485]] Wikidata name: Nanbu Fire Festival [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Fireworks Festival |name=Nanbu Fire Festival |english_name= |image=[[File:南部の火祭り 船灯籠 - Panoramio 57392817.jpg|300px]] |caption=Boat lanterns at the Nanbu Fire Festival |alias= |official_name=Nanbu Fire Festival |former_name= |schedule=[[August 15]] (previously [[August 16]] during the Shōwa era) |first_event=[[1960]] (Shōwa 35), August 16 |venue=[[:en:Nanbu, Yamanashi|Nanbu, Yamanashi]], [[:en:Fuji River|Fuji River]] riverside near {{ill|Nanbu Bridge (Fuji River)|ja|南部橋 (富士川)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanbu Bridge (Fuji River)|1=Nanbu bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11408565}}, [[:en:Minamikoma District, Yamanashi|Minamikoma District, Yamanashi]] |fireworks_count=3,000 |organizer=Nanbu Fire Festival Executive Committee |support= |sponsors= |cooperation= |management= |firework_type=Ni-shakudama (two-foot diameter shell) |attendance=40,000 |attendance_year=2014 |nearest_station=[[JR Central]] [[:en:Minobu Line|Minobu Line]], [[:en:Utsubuna Station|Utsubuna Station]] |shuttle_bus= |url=http://www.town.nanbu.yamanashi.jp/kankou/omatsuri/himatsuri/yurai.html |external_link_name=About the Nanbu Fire Festival |remarks= }} '''Nanbu Fire Festival''' (南部の火祭り) is a traditional summer festival held in [[:en:Nanbu, Yamanashi|Nanbu, Yamanashi]], [[:en:Minamikoma District, Yamanashi|Minamikoma District, Yamanashi]], during the [[:en:Obon|Obon]] season.
== Main Events == ;Torch Throwing Participants aim torches at a "beehive" (a straw-filled basket) mounted atop a tall pole, spinning and throwing the torches to ignite it. Once burned, the pole is shaken to release the remains. The tradition is similar to the {{ill|Karigane Festival|ja|かりがね祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karigane Festival|1=Karigane Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11264366}} held in [[:en:Fuji, Shizuoka|Fuji, Shizuoka]]. Before World War II, this event spanned three nights (August 14–16) and used split cedar logs tied with bamboo hoops. Modern torches are made from reused cloth soaked in waste oil or kerosene and secured with wire. ;Giant Torches Old tōba (wooden memorial plaques) from local temples are stacked to create large torches. These are lit around 7 PM following sutra chanting by monks. ;One Hundred and Eight Fires Symbolizing the cleansing of humanity's 108 earthly desires (bonnō), the ritual also serves as a memorial for the dead and a pest control measure to drive away rice planthopper pests (unja). Cone-shaped bonfires are placed along both banks of the [[:en:Fuji River|Fuji River]], covering approximately 2 km. All 108 fires are lit simultaneously at 8 PM following a siren. ;Fireworks Following the lighting of the 108 fires, a fireworks display featuring 3,000 shells, including two-foot diameter shells, illuminates the sky. ;Additional Activities The main venue on the Mutsumiai side features events such as karaoke contests and food stalls. However, due to financial constraints in Nanbu Town, the scale of the festival has been reduced in recent years.
== Transportation == ;Rail * To the Sakae-side venue: A 2-minute walk from [[:en:Utsubuna Station|Utsubuna Station]] on the [[:en:Minobu Line|Minobu Line]]. Temporary trains operate during the festival, similar to those for the {{ill|Shinmei Fireworks Festival|ja|神明の花火大会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei Fireworks Festival|1=Shinmei Fireworks Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590283}}. Food stalls offering classics like yakisoba, takoyaki, and jumbo sausages are present, but additional snacks and drinks can be purchased at nearby convenience stores (e.g., a Daily Yamazaki) or local shops. * To the Mutsumiai-side main venue: A 10-minute walk from Utsubuna Station, crossing the {{ill|Nanbu Bridge (Fuji River)|ja|南部橋 (富士川)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanbu Bridge (Fuji River)|1=Nanbu bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11408565}}. While the 2011 Completion tier 2]][[Category: of a new bridge widened the pedestrian path, traffic restrictions may apply during busy times. * Additional services: Temporary trains or extended train schedules are available between [[:en:Fuji Station|Fuji Station]], Utsubuna Station, and [[:en:Minobu Station|Minobu Station]]. Temporary train schedules are typically not listed in commercial timetables but are announced via [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]'s official website or flyers distributed around the same time as the Shinmei Fireworks Festival announcements. ;Car * Guidance signs are posted along [[:en:Japan National Route 52|National Route 52 (Japan)]] and {{ill|Shizuoka-Yamanashi Prefectural Route 10|ja|静岡県道・山梨県道10号富士川身延線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuoka-Yamanashi Prefectural Route 10|1=Shizuoka Prefectural Road and Yamanashi Prefectural Road Route 10|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11663557}}. Parking is available near the main venue on the Mutsumiai side but requires using designated lots due to traffic restrictions. Parking areas include locations near Utsubuna Station and along the Fuji River embankments. Note that the parking lot at Utsubuna Station, usually free, charges fees on the festival day.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Nanbu Fire Festival}} * [http://www.town.nanbu.yamanashi.jp/kankou/omatsuri/himatsuri/yurai.html Nanbu Fire Festival (Nanbu Town Hall)]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Nanbu Fire Festival]][[ja:南部の火祭り]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11408485]] Wikidata name: Nanbu Fire Festival [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Fireworks Festival |name=Nanbu Fire Festival |english_name= |image=[[File:南部の火祭り 船灯籠 - Panoramio 57392817.jpg|300px]] |caption=Boat lanterns at the Nanbu Fire Festival |alias= |official_name=Nanbu Fire Festival |former_name= |schedule=[[August 15]] (previously [[August 16]] during the Shōwa era) |first_event=[[1960]] (Shōwa 35), August 16 |venue=[[:en:Nanbu, Yamanashi|Nanbu, Yamanashi]], [[:en:Fuji River|Fuji River]] riverside near {{ill|Nanbu Bridge (Fuji River)|ja|南部橋 (富士川)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanbu Bridge (Fuji River)|1=Nanbu bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11408565}}, [[:en:Minamikoma District, Yamanashi|Minamikoma District, Yamanashi]] |fireworks_count=3,000 |organizer=Nanbu Fire Festival Executive Committee |support= |sponsors= |cooperation= |management= |firework_type=Ni-shakudama (two-foot diameter shell) |attendance=40,000 |attendance_year=2014 |nearest_station=[[JR Central]] [[:en:Minobu Line|Minobu Line]], [[:en:Utsubuna Station|Utsubuna Station]] |shuttle_bus= |url=http://www.town.nanbu.yamanashi.jp/kankou/omatsuri/himatsuri/yurai.html |external_link_name=About the Nanbu Fire Festival |remarks= }} '''Nanbu Fire Festival''' (南部の火祭り) is a traditional summer festival held in [[:en:Nanbu, Yamanashi|Nanbu, Yamanashi]], [[:en:Minamikoma District, Yamanashi|Minamikoma District, Yamanashi]], during the [[:en:Obon|Obon]] season.
== Main Events == ;Torch Throwing Participants aim torches at a "beehive" (a straw-filled basket) mounted atop a tall pole, spinning and throwing the torches to ignite it. Once burned, the pole is shaken to release the remains. The tradition is similar to the {{ill|Karigane Festival|ja|かりがね祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karigane Festival|1=Karigane Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11264366}} held in [[:en:Fuji, Shizuoka|Fuji, Shizuoka]]. Before World War II, this event spanned three nights (August 14–16) and used split cedar logs tied with bamboo hoops. Modern torches are made from reused cloth soaked in waste oil or kerosene and secured with wire. ;Giant Torches Old tōba (wooden memorial plaques) from local temples are stacked to create large torches. These are lit around 7 PM following sutra chanting by monks. ;One Hundred and Eight Fires Symbolizing the cleansing of humanity's 108 earthly desires (bonnō), the ritual also serves as a memorial for the dead and a pest control measure to drive away rice planthopper pests (unja). Cone-shaped bonfires are placed along both banks of the [[:en:Fuji River|Fuji River]], covering approximately 2 km. All 108 fires are lit simultaneously at 8 PM following a siren. ;Fireworks Following the lighting of the 108 fires, a fireworks display featuring 3,000 shells, including two-foot diameter shells, illuminates the sky. ;Additional Activities The main venue on the Mutsumiai side features events such as karaoke contests and food stalls. However, due to financial constraints in Nanbu Town, the scale of the festival has been reduced in recent years.
== Transportation == ;Rail * To the Sakae-side venue: A 2-minute walk from [[:en:Utsubuna Station|Utsubuna Station]] on the [[:en:Minobu Line|Minobu Line]]. Temporary trains operate during the festival, similar to those for the {{ill|Shinmei Fireworks Festival|ja|神明の花火大会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei Fireworks Festival|1=Shinmei Fireworks Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590283}}. Food stalls offering classics like yakisoba, takoyaki, and jumbo sausages are present, but additional snacks and drinks can be purchased at nearby convenience stores (e.g., a Daily Yamazaki) or local shops. * To the Mutsumiai-side main venue: A 10-minute walk from Utsubuna Station, crossing the {{ill|Nanbu Bridge (Fuji River)|ja|南部橋 (富士川)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanbu Bridge (Fuji River)|1=Nanbu bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11408565}}. While the 2011 Completion tier 2]][[Category: of a new bridge widened the pedestrian path, traffic restrictions may apply during busy times. * Additional services: Temporary trains or extended train schedules are available between [[:en:Fuji Station|Fuji Station]], Utsubuna Station, and [[:en:Minobu Station|Minobu Station]]. Temporary train schedules are typically not listed in commercial timetables but are announced via [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]'s official website or flyers distributed around the same time as the Shinmei Fireworks Festival announcements. ;Car * Guidance signs are posted along [[:en:Japan National Route 52|National Route 52 (Japan)]] and {{ill|Shizuoka-Yamanashi Prefectural Route 10|ja|静岡県道・山梨県道10号富士川身延線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuoka-Yamanashi Prefectural Route 10|1=Shizuoka Prefectural Road and Yamanashi Prefectural Road Route 10|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11663557}}. Parking is available near the main venue on the Mutsumiai side but requires using designated lots due to traffic restrictions. Parking areas include locations near Utsubuna Station and along the Fuji River embankments. Note that the parking lot at Utsubuna Station, usually free, charges fees on the festival day.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Nanbu Fire Festival}} * [http://www.town.nanbu.yamanashi.jp/kankou/omatsuri/himatsuri/yurai.html Nanbu Fire Festival (Nanbu Town Hall)]

<!--Nambu Fire Festival--><!--Nambu Fire Festival--><!--Nambu Fire Festival--><!--Nambu Fire Festival--><!--Nambu Fire Festival--><!--Nambu Fire Festival--><!--Nambu Fire Festival--><!--Nambu Fire Festival--><!--Nambu Fire Festival--> [[Category:Nanbu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:Fujikawa River System]] [[Category:Festivals in Yamanashi Prefecture]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Nanbu Fire Festival}} {{wikidata link|Q11408485}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Nanbu Fire Festival]][[ja:南部の火祭り]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11408485]] Wikidata name: Nanbu Fire Festival [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Fireworks Festival |name=Nanbu Fire Festival |english_name= |image=[[File:南部の火祭り 船灯籠 - Panoramio 57392817.jpg|300px]] |caption=Boat lanterns at the Nanbu Fire Festival |alias= |official_name=Nanbu Fire Festival |former_name= |schedule=[[August 15]] (previously [[August 16]] during the Shōwa era) |first_event=[[1960]] (Shōwa 35), August 16 |venue=[[:en:Nanbu, Yamanashi|Nanbu, Yamanashi]], [[:en:Fuji River|Fuji River]] riverside near {{ill|Nanbu Bridge (Fuji River)|ja|南部橋 (富士川)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanbu Bridge (Fuji River)|1=Nanbu bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11408565}}, [[:en:Minamikoma District, Yamanashi|Minamikoma District, Yamanashi]] |fireworks_count=3,000 |organizer=Nanbu Fire Festival Executive Committee |support= |sponsors= |cooperation= |management= |firework_type=Ni-shakudama (two-foot diameter shell) |attendance=40,000 |attendance_year=2014 |nearest_station=[[JR Central]] [[:en:Minobu Line|Minobu Line]], [[:en:Utsubuna Station|Utsubuna Station]] |shuttle_bus= |url=http://www.town.nanbu.yamanashi.jp/kankou/omatsuri/himatsuri/yurai.html |external_link_name=About the Nanbu Fire Festival |remarks= }} '''Nanbu Fire Festival''' (南部の火祭り) is a traditional summer festival held in [[:en:Nanbu, Yamanashi|Nanbu, Yamanashi]], [[:en:Minamikoma District, Yamanashi|Minamikoma District, Yamanashi]], during the [[:en:Obon|Obon]] season.
== Main Events == ;Torch Throwing Participants aim torches at a "beehive" (a straw-filled basket) mounted atop a tall pole, spinning and throwing the torches to ignite it. Once burned, the pole is shaken to release the remains. The tradition is similar to the {{ill|Karigane Festival|ja|かりがね祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karigane Festival|1=Karigane Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11264366}} held in [[:en:Fuji, Shizuoka|Fuji, Shizuoka]]. Before World War II, this event spanned three nights (August 14–16) and used split cedar logs tied with bamboo hoops. Modern torches are made from reused cloth soaked in waste oil or kerosene and secured with wire. ;Giant Torches Old tōba (wooden memorial plaques) from local temples are stacked to create large torches. These are lit around 7 PM following sutra chanting by monks. ;One Hundred and Eight Fires Symbolizing the cleansing of humanity's 108 earthly desires (bonnō), the ritual also serves as a memorial for the dead and a pest control measure to drive away rice planthopper pests (unja). Cone-shaped bonfires are placed along both banks of the [[:en:Fuji River|Fuji River]], covering approximately 2 km. All 108 fires are lit simultaneously at 8 PM following a siren. ;Fireworks Following the lighting of the 108 fires, a fireworks display featuring 3,000 shells, including two-foot diameter shells, illuminates the sky. ;Additional Activities The main venue on the Mutsumiai side features events such as karaoke contests and food stalls. However, due to financial constraints in Nanbu Town, the scale of the festival has been reduced in recent years.
== Transportation == ;Rail * To the Sakae-side venue: A 2-minute walk from [[:en:Utsubuna Station|Utsubuna Station]] on the [[:en:Minobu Line|Minobu Line]]. Temporary trains operate during the festival, similar to those for the {{ill|Shinmei Fireworks Festival|ja|神明の花火大会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei Fireworks Festival|1=Shinmei Fireworks Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590283}}. Food stalls offering classics like yakisoba, takoyaki, and jumbo sausages are present, but additional snacks and drinks can be purchased at nearby convenience stores (e.g., a Daily Yamazaki) or local shops. * To the Mutsumiai-side main venue: A 10-minute walk from Utsubuna Station, crossing the {{ill|Nanbu Bridge (Fuji River)|ja|南部橋 (富士川)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanbu Bridge (Fuji River)|1=Nanbu bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11408565}}. While the 2011 Completion tier 2]][[Category: of a new bridge widened the pedestrian path, traffic restrictions may apply during busy times. * Additional services: Temporary trains or extended train schedules are available between [[:en:Fuji Station|Fuji Station]], Utsubuna Station, and [[:en:Minobu Station|Minobu Station]]. Temporary train schedules are typically not listed in commercial timetables but are announced via [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]'s official website or flyers distributed around the same time as the Shinmei Fireworks Festival announcements. ;Car * Guidance signs are posted along [[:en:Japan National Route 52|National Route 52 (Japan)]] and {{ill|Shizuoka-Yamanashi Prefectural Route 10|ja|静岡県道・山梨県道10号富士川身延線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuoka-Yamanashi Prefectural Route 10|1=Shizuoka Prefectural Road and Yamanashi Prefectural Road Route 10|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11663557}}. Parking is available near the main venue on the Mutsumiai side but requires using designated lots due to traffic restrictions. Parking areas include locations near Utsubuna Station and along the Fuji River embankments. Note that the parking lot at Utsubuna Station, usually free, charges fees on the festival day.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Nanbu Fire Festival}} * [http://www.town.nanbu.yamanashi.jp/kankou/omatsuri/himatsuri/yurai.html Nanbu Fire Festival (Nanbu Town Hall)]

<!--Nambu Fire Festival--><!--Nambu Fire Festival--><!--Nambu Fire Festival--><!--Nambu Fire Festival--><!--Nambu Fire Festival--><!--Nambu Fire Festival--><!--Nambu Fire Festival--><!--Nambu Fire Festival--><!--Nambu Fire Festival--> [[Category:Nanbu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:Fujikawa River System]] [[Category:Festivals in Yamanashi Prefecture]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Nanbu Fire Festival}} {{wikidata link|Q11408485}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Nanbu Fire Festival]][[ja:南部の火祭り]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Nanbu Fire Festival]][[ja:南部の火祭り]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Nanbu Fire Festival]][[ja:南部の火祭り]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11408485]] Wikidata name: Nanbu Fire Festival [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Fireworks Festival |name=Nanbu Fire Festival |english_name= |image=[[File:南部の火祭り 船灯籠 - Panoramio 57392817.jpg|300px]] |caption=Boat lanterns at the Nanbu Fire Festival |alias= |official_name=Nanbu Fire Festival |former_name= |schedule=[[August 15]] (previously [[August 16]] during the Shōwa era) |first_event=[[1960]] (Shōwa 35), August 16 |venue=[[:en:Nanbu, Yamanashi|Nanbu, Yamanashi]], [[:en:Fuji River|Fuji River]] riverside near {{ill|Nanbu Bridge (Fuji River)|ja|南部橋 (富士川)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanbu Bridge (Fuji River)|1=Nanbu bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11408565}}, [[:en:Minamikoma District, Yamanashi|Minamikoma District, Yamanashi]] |fireworks_count=3,000 |organizer=Nanbu Fire Festival Executive Committee |support= |sponsors= |cooperation= |management= |firework_type=Ni-shakudama (two-foot diameter shell) |attendance=40,000 |attendance_year=2014 |nearest_station=[[JR Central]] [[:en:Minobu Line|Minobu Line]], [[:en:Utsubuna Station|Utsubuna Station]] |shuttle_bus= |url=http://www.town.nanbu.yamanashi.jp/kankou/omatsuri/himatsuri/yurai.html |external_link_name=About the Nanbu Fire Festival |remarks= }} '''Nanbu Fire Festival''' (南部の火祭り) is a traditional summer festival held in [[:en:Nanbu, Yamanashi|Nanbu, Yamanashi]], [[:en:Minamikoma District, Yamanashi|Minamikoma District, Yamanashi]], during the [[:en:Obon|Obon]] season.
== Main Events == ;Torch Throwing Participants aim torches at a "beehive" (a straw-filled basket) mounted atop a tall pole, spinning and throwing the torches to ignite it. Once burned, the pole is shaken to release the remains. The tradition is similar to the {{ill|Karigane Festival|ja|かりがね祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karigane Festival|1=Karigane Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11264366}} held in [[:en:Fuji, Shizuoka|Fuji, Shizuoka]]. Before World War II, this event spanned three nights (August 14–16) and used split cedar logs tied with bamboo hoops. Modern torches are made from reused cloth soaked in waste oil or kerosene and secured with wire. ;Giant Torches Old tōba (wooden memorial plaques) from local temples are stacked to create large torches. These are lit around 7 PM following sutra chanting by monks. ;One Hundred and Eight Fires Symbolizing the cleansing of humanity's 108 earthly desires (bonnō), the ritual also serves as a memorial for the dead and a pest control measure to drive away rice planthopper pests (unja). Cone-shaped bonfires are placed along both banks of the [[:en:Fuji River|Fuji River]], covering approximately 2 km. All 108 fires are lit simultaneously at 8 PM following a siren. ;Fireworks Following the lighting of the 108 fires, a fireworks display featuring 3,000 shells, including two-foot diameter shells, illuminates the sky. ;Additional Activities The main venue on the Mutsumiai side features events such as karaoke contests and food stalls. However, due to financial constraints in Nanbu Town, the scale of the festival has been reduced in recent years.
== Transportation == ;Rail * To the Sakae-side venue: A 2-minute walk from [[:en:Utsubuna Station|Utsubuna Station]] on the [[:en:Minobu Line|Minobu Line]]. Temporary trains operate during the festival, similar to those for the {{ill|Shinmei Fireworks Festival|ja|神明の花火大会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei Fireworks Festival|1=Shinmei Fireworks Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590283}}. Food stalls offering classics like yakisoba, takoyaki, and jumbo sausages are present, but additional snacks and drinks can be purchased at nearby convenience stores (e.g., a Daily Yamazaki) or local shops. * To the Mutsumiai-side main venue: A 10-minute walk from Utsubuna Station, crossing the {{ill|Nanbu Bridge (Fuji River)|ja|南部橋 (富士川)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanbu Bridge (Fuji River)|1=Nanbu bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11408565}}. While the 2011 Completion tier 2]][[Category: of a new bridge widened the pedestrian path, traffic restrictions may apply during busy times. * Additional services: Temporary trains or extended train schedules are available between [[:en:Fuji Station|Fuji Station]], Utsubuna Station, and [[:en:Minobu Station|Minobu Station]]. Temporary train schedules are typically not listed in commercial timetables but are announced via [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]'s official website or flyers distributed around the same time as the Shinmei Fireworks Festival announcements. ;Car * Guidance signs are posted along [[:en:Japan National Route 52|National Route 52 (Japan)]] and {{ill|Shizuoka-Yamanashi Prefectural Route 10|ja|静岡県道・山梨県道10号富士川身延線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuoka-Yamanashi Prefectural Route 10|1=Shizuoka Prefectural Road and Yamanashi Prefectural Road Route 10|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11663557}}. Parking is available near the main venue on the Mutsumiai side but requires using designated lots due to traffic restrictions. Parking areas include locations near Utsubuna Station and along the Fuji River embankments. Note that the parking lot at Utsubuna Station, usually free, charges fees on the festival day.
== References == {{Reflist}}

[[ja:南部の火祭り]]
<!--Nambu Fire Festival--><!--Nambu Fire Festival--><!--Nambu Fire Festival--><!--Nambu Fire Festival--><!--Nambu Fire Festival--><!--Nambu Fire Festival--><!--Nambu Fire Festival--><!--Nambu Fire Festival--><!--Nambu Fire Festival--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Nanbu Fire Festival}} {{wikidata link|Q11408485}} [[Category: of a new bridge widened the pedestrian path, traffic restrictions may apply during busy times. * Additional services: Temporary trains or extended train schedules are available between [[:en:Fuji Station|Fuji Station]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Festivals in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Fujikawa River System]] [[Category:Nanbu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nanbu Fire Festival]][[ja:南部の火祭り]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nanbu Fire Festival]][[ja:南部の火祭り]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Nanbu Fire Festival]][[ja:南部の火祭り]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11408485]] Wikidata name: Nanbu Fire Festival [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Fireworks Festival |name=Nanbu Fire Festival |english_name= |image=[[File:南部の火祭り 船灯籠 - Panoramio 57392817.jpg|300px]] |caption=Boat lanterns at the Nanbu Fire Festival |alias= |official_name=Nanbu Fire Festival |former_name= |schedule=[[August 15]] (previously [[August 16]] during the Shōwa era) |first_event=[[1960]] (Shōwa 35), August 16 |venue=[[:en:Nanbu, Yamanashi|Nanbu, Yamanashi]], [[:en:Fuji River|Fuji River]] riverside near {{ill|Nanbu Bridge (Fuji River)|ja|南部橋 (富士川)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanbu Bridge (Fuji River)|1=Nanbu bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11408565}}, [[:en:Minamikoma District, Yamanashi|Minamikoma District, Yamanashi]] |fireworks_count=3,000 |organizer=Nanbu Fire Festival Executive Committee |support= |sponsors= |cooperation= |management= |firework_type=Ni-shakudama (two-foot diameter shell) |attendance=40,000 |attendance_year=2014 |nearest_station=[[JR Central]] [[:en:Minobu Line|Minobu Line]], [[:en:Utsubuna Station|Utsubuna Station]] |shuttle_bus= |url=http://www.town.nanbu.yamanashi.jp/kankou/omatsuri/himatsuri/yurai.html |external_link_name=About the Nanbu Fire Festival |remarks= }} '''Nanbu Fire Festival''' (南部の火祭り) is a traditional summer festival held in [[:en:Nanbu, Yamanashi|Nanbu, Yamanashi]], [[:en:Minamikoma District, Yamanashi|Minamikoma District, Yamanashi]], during the [[:en:Obon|Obon]] season.
== Main Events == ;Torch Throwing Participants aim torches at a "beehive" (a straw-filled basket) mounted atop a tall pole, spinning and throwing the torches to ignite it. Once burned, the pole is shaken to release the remains. The tradition is similar to the {{ill|Karigane Festival|ja|かりがね祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karigane Festival|1=Karigane Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11264366}} held in [[:en:Fuji, Shizuoka|Fuji, Shizuoka]]. Before World War II, this event spanned three nights (August 14–16) and used split cedar logs tied with bamboo hoops. Modern torches are made from reused cloth soaked in waste oil or kerosene and secured with wire. ;Giant Torches Old tōba (wooden memorial plaques) from local temples are stacked to create large torches. These are lit around 7 PM following sutra chanting by monks. ;One Hundred and Eight Fires Symbolizing the cleansing of humanity's 108 earthly desires (bonnō), the ritual also serves as a memorial for the dead and a pest control measure to drive away rice planthopper pests (unja). Cone-shaped bonfires are placed along both banks of the [[:en:Fuji River|Fuji River]], covering approximately 2 km. All 108 fires are lit simultaneously at 8 PM following a siren. ;Fireworks Following the lighting of the 108 fires, a fireworks display featuring 3,000 shells, including two-foot diameter shells, illuminates the sky. ;Additional Activities The main venue on the Mutsumiai side features events such as karaoke contests and food stalls. However, due to financial constraints in Nanbu Town, the scale of the festival has been reduced in recent years.
== Transportation == ;Rail * To the Sakae-side venue: A 2-minute walk from [[:en:Utsubuna Station|Utsubuna Station]] on the [[:en:Minobu Line|Minobu Line]]. Temporary trains operate during the festival, similar to those for the {{ill|Shinmei Fireworks Festival|ja|神明の花火大会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei Fireworks Festival|1=Shinmei Fireworks Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590283}}. Food stalls offering classics like yakisoba, takoyaki, and jumbo sausages are present, but additional snacks and drinks can be purchased at nearby convenience stores (e.g., a Daily Yamazaki) or local shops. * To the Mutsumiai-side main venue: A 10-minute walk from Utsubuna Station, crossing the {{ill|Nanbu Bridge (Fuji River)|ja|南部橋 (富士川)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanbu Bridge (Fuji River)|1=Nanbu bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11408565}}. While the 2011 Completion tier 2]], Utsubuna Station, and [[:en:Minobu Station|Minobu Station]]. Temporary train schedules are typically not listed in commercial timetables but are announced via [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]'s official website or flyers distributed around the same time as the Shinmei Fireworks Festival announcements. ;Car * Guidance signs are posted along [[:en:Japan National Route 52|National Route 52 (Japan)]] and {{ill|Shizuoka-Yamanashi Prefectural Route 10|ja|静岡県道・山梨県道10号富士川身延線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuoka-Yamanashi Prefectural Route 10|1=Shizuoka Prefectural Road and Yamanashi Prefectural Road Route 10|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11663557}}. Parking is available near the main venue on the Mutsumiai side but requires using designated lots due to traffic restrictions. Parking areas include locations near Utsubuna Station and along the Fuji River embankments. Note that the parking lot at Utsubuna Station, usually free, charges fees on the festival day.
== References == {{Reflist}}

All 108 fires are lit simultaneously at 8 PM following a siren. ;Fireworks Following the lighting of the 108 fires, a fireworks display featuring 3,000 shells, including two-foot diameter shells, illuminates the sky. ;Additional Activities The main venue on the Mutsumiai side features events such as karaoke contests and food stalls. However, due to financial constraints in Nanbu Town, the scale of the festival has been reduced in recent years.
== Transportation == ;Rail * To the Sakae-side venue: A 2-minute walk from {{ill|Utsubuna Station|en|Utsubuna Station|WD=Q7903273|lt=Utsubuna Station}} on the {{ill|Minobu Line|en|Minobu Line|WD=Q162747|lt=Minobu Line}}. Temporary trains operate during the festival, similar to those for the {{ill|Shinmei Fireworks Festival|ja|神明の花火大会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei Fireworks Festival|1=Shinmei Fireworks Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590283}}. Food stalls offering classics like yakisoba, takoyaki, and jumbo sausages are present, but additional snacks and drinks can be purchased at nearby convenience stores (e.g., a Daily Yamazaki) or local shops. * To the Mutsumiai-side main venue: A 10-minute walk from Utsubuna Station, crossing the {{ill|Nanbu Bridge (Fuji River)|ja|南部橋 (富士川)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanbu Bridge (Fuji River)|1=Nanbu bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11408565}}. While the 2011 Completion tier 2]], Utsubuna Station, and {{ill|Minobu Station|en|Minobu Station|WD=Q5356755|lt=Minobu Station}}. Temporary train schedules are typically not listed in commercial timetables but are announced via {{ill|Central Japan Railway Company|en|Central Japan Railway Company|WD=Q513679|lt=JR Central}}'s official website or flyers distributed around the same time as the Shinmei Fireworks Festival announcements. ;Car * Guidance signs are posted along {{ill|Japan National Route 52|en|Japan National Route 52|WD=Q6157734|lt=National Route 52 (Japan)}} and {{ill|Shizuoka-Yamanashi Prefectural Route 10|ja|静岡県道・山梨県道10号富士川身延線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuoka-Yamanashi Prefectural Route 10|1=Shizuoka Prefectural Road and Yamanashi Prefectural Road Route 10|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11663557}}. Parking is available near the main venue on the Mutsumiai side but requires using designated lots due to traffic restrictions. Parking areas include locations near Utsubuna Station and along the Fuji River embankments. Note that the parking lot at Utsubuna Station, usually free, charges fees on the festival day.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nanbu Fire Festival]][[ja:南部の火祭り]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nanbu Fire Festival]][[ja:南部の火祭り]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Nanbu Fire Festival]][[ja:南部の火祭り]]
{{Fireworks Festival |name=Nanbu Fire Festival |english_name= |image=[[File:南部の火祭り 船灯籠 - Panoramio 57392817.jpg|300px]] |caption=Boat lanterns at the Nanbu Fire Festival |alias= |official_name=Nanbu Fire Festival |former_name= |schedule=[[August 15]] (previously [[August 16]] during the Shōwa era) |first_event=[[1960]] (Shōwa 35), August 16 |venue={{ill|Nanbu, Yamanashi|en|Nanbu, Yamanashi|WD=Q1204802|lt=Nanbu, Yamanashi}}, {{ill|Fuji River|en|Fuji River|WD=Q596110|lt=Fuji River}} riverside near {{ill|Nanbu Bridge (Fuji River)|ja|南部橋 (富士川)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanbu Bridge (Fuji River)|1=Nanbu bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11408565}}, {{ill|Minamikoma District, Yamanashi|en|Minamikoma District, Yamanashi|WD=Q1131437|lt=Minamikoma District, Yamanashi}} |fireworks_count=3,000 |organizer=Nanbu Fire Festival Executive Committee |support= |sponsors= |cooperation= |management= |firework_type=Ni-shakudama (two-foot diameter shell) |attendance=40,000 |attendance_year=2014 |nearest_station=[[JR Central]] {{ill|Minobu Line|en|Minobu Line|WD=Q162747|lt=Minobu Line}}, {{ill|Utsubuna Station|en|Utsubuna Station|WD=Q7903273|lt=Utsubuna Station}} |shuttle_bus= |url=http://www.town.nanbu.yamanashi.jp/kankou/omatsuri/himatsuri/yurai.html |external_link_name=About the Nanbu Fire Festival |remarks= }} '''Nanbu Fire Festival''' (南部の火祭り) is a traditional summer festival held in {{ill|Nanbu, Yamanashi|en|Nanbu, Yamanashi|WD=Q1204802|lt=Nanbu, Yamanashi}}, {{ill|Minamikoma District, Yamanashi|en|Minamikoma District, Yamanashi|WD=Q1131437|lt=Minamikoma District, Yamanashi}}, during the {{ill|Obon|en|Obon|WD=Q1154888|lt=Obon}} season.
== Main Events == ;Torch Throwing Participants aim torches at a "beehive" (a straw-filled basket) mounted atop a tall pole, spinning and throwing the torches to ignite it. Once burned, the pole is shaken to release the remains. The tradition is similar to the {{ill|Karigane Festival|ja|かりがね祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karigane Festival|1=Karigane Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11264366}} held in {{ill|Fuji, Shizuoka|en|Fuji, Shizuoka|WD=Q328613|lt=Fuji, Shizuoka}}. Before World War II, this event spanned three nights (August 14–16) and used split cedar logs tied with bamboo hoops. Modern torches are made from reused cloth soaked in waste oil or kerosene and secured with wire. ;Giant Torches Old tōba (wooden memorial plaques) from local temples are stacked to create large torches. These are lit around 7 PM following sutra chanting by monks. ;One Hundred and Eight Fires Symbolizing the cleansing of humanity's 108 earthly desires (bonnō), the ritual also serves as a memorial for the dead and a pest control measure to drive away rice planthopper pests (unja). Cone-shaped bonfires are placed along both banks of the {{ill|Fuji River|en|Fuji River|WD=Q596110|lt=Fuji River}}, covering approximately 2 km. All 108 fires are lit simultaneously at 8 PM following a siren. ;Fireworks Following the lighting of the 108 fires, a fireworks display featuring 3,000 shells, including two-foot diameter shells, illuminates the sky. ;Additional Activities The main venue on the Mutsumiai side features events such as karaoke contests and food stalls. However, due to financial constraints in Nanbu Town, the scale of the festival has been reduced in recent years.
== Transportation == ;Rail * To the Sakae-side venue: A 2-minute walk from {{ill|Utsubuna Station|en|Utsubuna Station|WD=Q7903273|lt=Utsubuna Station}} on the {{ill|Minobu Line|en|Minobu Line|WD=Q162747|lt=Minobu Line}}. Temporary trains operate during the festival, similar to those for the {{ill|Shinmei Fireworks Festival|ja|神明の花火大会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei Fireworks Festival|1=Shinmei Fireworks Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590283}}. Food stalls offering classics like yakisoba, takoyaki, and jumbo sausages are present, but additional snacks and drinks can be purchased at nearby convenience stores (e.g., a Daily Yamazaki) or local shops. * To the Mutsumiai-side main venue: A 10-minute walk from Utsubuna Station, crossing the {{ill|Nanbu Bridge (Fuji River)|ja|南部橋 (富士川)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanbu Bridge (Fuji River)|1=Nanbu bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11408565}}. While the 2011 Completion tier 2]], Utsubuna Station, and {{ill|Minobu Station|en|Minobu Station|WD=Q5356755|lt=Minobu Station}}. Temporary train schedules are typically not listed in commercial timetables but are announced via {{ill|Central Japan Railway Company|en|Central Japan Railway Company|WD=Q513679|lt=JR Central}}'s official website or flyers distributed around the same time as the Shinmei Fireworks Festival announcements. ;Car * Guidance signs are posted along {{ill|Japan National Route 52|en|Japan National Route 52|WD=Q6157734|lt=National Route 52 (Japan)}} and {{ill|Shizuoka-Yamanashi Prefectural Route 10|ja|静岡県道・山梨県道10号富士川身延線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuoka-Yamanashi Prefectural Route 10|1=Shizuoka Prefectural Road and Yamanashi Prefectural Road Route 10|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11663557}}. Parking is available near the main venue on the Mutsumiai side but requires using designated lots due to traffic restrictions. Parking areas include locations near Utsubuna Station and along the Fuji River embankments. Note that the parking lot at Utsubuna Station, usually free, charges fees on the festival day.

{{Fireworks Festival |name=Nanbu Fire Festival |english_name= |image=[[File:南部の火祭り 船灯籠 - Panoramio 57392817.jpg|300px]] |caption=Boat lanterns at the Nanbu Fire Festival |alias= |official_name=Nanbu Fire Festival |former_name= |schedule=[[August 15]] (previously [[August 16]] during the Shōwa era) |first_event=[[1960]] (Shōwa 35), August 16 |venue={{ill|Nanbu, Yamanashi|en|Nanbu, Yamanashi|WD=Q1204802|lt=Nanbu, Yamanashi}}, {{ill|Fuji River|en|Fuji River|WD=Q596110|lt=Fuji River}} riverside near {{ill|Nanbu Bridge (Fuji River)|ja|南部橋 (富士川)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanbu Bridge (Fuji River)|1=Nanbu bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11408565}}, {{ill|Minamikoma District, Yamanashi|en|Minamikoma District, Yamanashi|WD=Q1131437|lt=Minamikoma District, Yamanashi}} |fireworks_count=3,000 |organizer=Nanbu Fire Festival Executive Committee |support= |sponsors= |cooperation= |management= |firework_type=Ni-shakudama (two-foot diameter shell) |attendance=40,000 |attendance_year=2014 |nearest_station=[[JR Central]] {{ill|Minobu Line|en|Minobu Line|WD=Q162747|lt=Minobu Line}}, {{ill|Utsubuna Station|en|Utsubuna Station|WD=Q7903273|lt=Utsubuna Station}} |shuttle_bus= |url=http://www.town.nanbu.yamanashi.jp/kankou/omatsuri/himatsuri/yurai.html |external_link_name=About the Nanbu Fire Festival |remarks= }} '''Nanbu Fire Festival''' (南部の火祭り) is a traditional summer festival held in {{ill|Nanbu, Yamanashi|en|Nanbu, Yamanashi|WD=Q1204802|lt=Nanbu, Yamanashi}}, {{ill|Minamikoma District, Yamanashi|en|Minamikoma District, Yamanashi|WD=Q1131437|lt=Minamikoma District, Yamanashi}}, during the {{ill|Obon|en|Obon|WD=Q1154888|lt=Obon}} season.
== Main Events == ;Torch Throwing Participants aim torches at a "beehive" (a straw-filled basket) mounted atop a tall pole, spinning and throwing the torches to ignite it. Once burned, the pole is shaken to release the remains. The tradition is similar to the {{ill|Karigane Festival|ja|かりがね祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karigane Festival|1=Karigane Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11264366}} held in {{ill|Fuji, Shizuoka|en|Fuji, Shizuoka|WD=Q328613|lt=Fuji, Shizuoka}}. Before World War II, this event spanned three nights (August 14–16) and used split cedar logs tied with bamboo hoops. Modern torches are made from reused cloth soaked in waste oil or kerosene and secured with wire. ;Giant Torches Old tōba (wooden memorial plaques) from local temples are stacked to create large torches. These are lit around 7 PM following sutra chanting by monks. ;One Hundred and Eight Fires Symbolizing the cleansing of humanity's 108 earthly desires (bonnō), the ritual also serves as a memorial for the dead and a pest control measure to drive away rice planthopper pests (unja). Cone-shaped bonfires are placed along both banks of the {{ill|Fuji River|en|Fuji River|WD=Q596110|lt=Fuji River}}, covering approximately 2 km. All 108 fires are lit simultaneously at 8 PM following a siren. ;Fireworks Following the lighting of the 108 fires, a fireworks display featuring 3,000 shells, including two-foot diameter shells, illuminates the sky. ;Additional Activities The main venue on the Mutsumiai side features events such as karaoke contests and food stalls. However, due to financial constraints in Nanbu Town, the scale of the festival has been reduced in recent years.
== Transportation == ;Rail * To the Sakae-side venue: A 2-minute walk from {{ill|Utsubuna Station|en|Utsubuna Station|WD=Q7903273|lt=Utsubuna Station}} on the {{ill|Minobu Line|en|Minobu Line|WD=Q162747|lt=Minobu Line}}. Temporary trains operate during the festival, similar to those for the {{ill|Shinmei Fireworks Festival|ja|神明の花火大会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei Fireworks Festival|1=Shinmei Fireworks Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590283}}. Food stalls offering classics like yakisoba, takoyaki, and jumbo sausages are present, but additional snacks and drinks can be purchased at nearby convenience stores (e.g., a Daily Yamazaki) or local shops. * To the Mutsumiai-side main venue: A 10-minute walk from Utsubuna Station, crossing the {{ill|Nanbu Bridge (Fuji River)|ja|南部橋 (富士川)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanbu Bridge (Fuji River)|1=Nanbu bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11408565}}. While the 2011 Completion tier 2]], Utsubuna Station, and {{ill|Minobu Station|en|Minobu Station|WD=Q5356755|lt=Minobu Station}}. Temporary train schedules are typically not listed in commercial timetables but are announced via {{ill|Central Japan Railway Company|en|Central Japan Railway Company|WD=Q513679|lt=JR Central}}'s official website or flyers distributed around the same time as the Shinmei Fireworks Festival announcements. ;Car * Guidance signs are posted along {{ill|Japan National Route 52|en|Japan National Route 52|WD=Q6157734|lt=National Route 52 (Japan)}} and {{ill|Shizuoka-Yamanashi Prefectural Route 10|ja|静岡県道・山梨県道10号富士川身延線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuoka-Yamanashi Prefectural Route 10|1=Shizuoka Prefectural Road and Yamanashi Prefectural Road Route 10|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11663557}}. Parking is available near the main venue on the Mutsumiai side but requires using designated lots due to traffic restrictions. Parking areas include locations near Utsubuna Station and along the Fuji River embankments. Note that the parking lot at Utsubuna Station, usually free, charges fees on the festival day.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Nanbu Fire Festival}} * [http://www.town.nanbu.yamanashi.jp/kankou/omatsuri/himatsuri/yurai.html Nanbu Fire Festival (Nanbu Town Hall)]

The tradition is similar to the {{ill|Karigane Festival|ja|かりがね祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karigane Festival|1=Q11264366|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11264366}} held in {{ill|Fuji, Shizuoka|en|Fuji, Shizuoka|WD=Q328613|lt=Fuji, Shizuoka|1=Fuji, Shizuoka}}. Before World War II, this event spanned three nights (August 14–16) and used split cedar logs tied with bamboo hoops. Modern torches are made from reused cloth soaked in waste oil or kerosene and secured with wire. ;Giant Torches Old tōba (wooden memorial plaques) from local temples are stacked to create large torches. These are lit around 7 PM following sutra chanting by monks. ;One Hundred and Eight Fires Symbolizing the cleansing of humanity's 108 earthly desires (bonnō), the ritual also serves as a memorial for the dead and a pest control measure to drive away rice planthopper pests (unja). Cone-shaped bonfires are placed along both banks of the {{ill|Fuji River|en|Fuji River|WD=Q596110|lt=Fuji River|1=Fuji River}}, covering approximately 2 km. All 108 fires are lit simultaneously at 8 PM following a siren. ;Fireworks Following the lighting of the 108 fires, a fireworks display featuring 3,000 shells, including two-foot diameter shells, illuminates the sky. ;Additional Activities The main venue on the Mutsumiai side features events such as karaoke contests and food stalls. However, due to financial constraints in Nanbu Town, the scale of the festival has been reduced in recent years.
== Transportation == ;Rail * To the Sakae-side venue: A 2-minute walk from {{ill|Utsubuna Station|en|Utsubuna Station|WD=Q7903273|lt=Utsubuna Station|1=Utsubuna Station}} on the {{ill|Minobu Line|en|Minobu Line|WD=Q162747|lt=Minobu Line|1=Minobu Line}}. Temporary trains operate during the festival, similar to those for the {{ill|Shinmei Fireworks Festival|ja|神明の花火大会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei Fireworks Festival|1=Shinmei Fireworks Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590283}}. Food stalls offering classics like yakisoba, takoyaki, and jumbo sausages are present, but additional snacks and drinks can be purchased at nearby convenience stores (e.g., a Daily Yamazaki) or local shops. * To the Mutsumiai-side main venue: A 10-minute walk from Utsubuna Station, crossing the {{ill|Nanbu Bridge (Fuji River)|ja|南部橋 (富士川)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanbu Bridge (Fuji River)|1=Nanbu bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11408565}}. While the 2011 Completion tier 2]], Utsubuna Station, and {{ill|Minobu Station|en|Minobu Station|WD=Q5356755|lt=Minobu Station|1=Minobu Station}}. Temporary train schedules are typically not listed in commercial timetables but are announced via {{ill|Central Japan Railway Company|en|Central Japan Railway Company|WD=Q513679|lt=JR Central|1=Central Japan Railway Company}}'s official website or flyers distributed around the same time as the Shinmei Fireworks Festival announcements. ;Car * Guidance signs are posted along {{ill|Japan National Route 52|en|Japan National Route 52|WD=Q6157734|lt=National Route 52 (Japan)|1=Japan National Route 52}} and {{ill|Shizuoka-Yamanashi Prefectural Route 10|ja|静岡県道・山梨県道10号富士川身延線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuoka-Yamanashi Prefectural Route 10|1=Shizuoka Prefectural Road and Yamanashi Prefectural Road Route 10|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11663557}}. Parking is available near the main venue on the Mutsumiai side but requires using designated lots due to traffic restrictions. Parking areas include locations near Utsubuna Station and along the Fuji River embankments. Note that the parking lot at Utsubuna Station, usually free, charges fees on the festival day.
